[12:56:09 INF] Quick Setup (0.10.0.0) (https://github.com/fuse-digital/quick-setup)
FuseDigital.QuickSetup.Cli 0.10.0-release.0+Branch.release-v0.10.0.Sha.23546ce9fb35a334693e335e18d796f5a4adb7f7.23546ce9fb35a334693e335e18d796f5a4adb7f7
Copyright (c) Fuse Digital (PTY) LTD 2026

  add         Adds a file or folder pattern to be included, tracked, and pushed
              to the remote repository.

  checkout    Clones an existing QUP remote repository and checks out the
              specified branch as a local repository.

  clone       The clone command allows you to download a copy of a Git source
              repository to your local machine. It automatically saves the
              repository information and sets up synchronization, so that you
              can easily work with the repository across multiple machines.

  fetch       The fetch command clones or pulls all the repositories in the
              projects file in your local environment. If a repository does not
              exist locally, it will be cloned from the remote repository. If
              the repository already exists locally, a `git pull` will be
              performed to update it with the latest changes.

  init        Initializes a new local git repository and pushes it to a remote
              repository.

  install     Install a specific package, packages for the specified package
              manager, or all package managers if none is specified.

  link        Create symbolic links between files or folders

  sync        Keep your files up to date and synchronized across multiple
              machines.

  update      Updates all packages for the specified package manager, or all
              package managers if none is specified.

  help        Display more information on a specific command.

  version     Display version information.

USAGE:
Install packages for all package managers:
  qup install
Install packages for a specific package manager:
  qup install apt
Install and track a specific package.:
  qup install apt curl

  -v, --verbosity             (Default: Warning) Set the verbosity level.
                              Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and
                              Warning

  --help                      Display this help screen.

  --version                   Display version information.

  package-manager (pos. 0)    The package manager to install packages for (e.g.,
                              pip, npm).

  package (pos. 1)            The package to install and add to the package
                              manager repository.

staticCommands.name: add
staticCommands.description: Adds a file or folder pattern to be included, tracked, and pushed to the remote repository.

values.name: pattern
values.description: The file/folder pattern to be included.

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: checkout
staticCommands.description: Clones an existing QUP remote repository and checks out the specified branch as a local repository.

values.name: repository
values.description: The URL of the remote repository to clone.

values.name: branch
values.description: The name of the branch to check out.

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: clone
staticCommands.description: The clone command allows you to download a copy of a Git source repository to your local machine. It automatically saves the repository information and sets up synchronization, so that you can easily work with the repository across multiple machines.

values.name: repository
values.description: The URL of the repository to clone.

values.name: path
values.description: The local path where the repository will be cloned. This can be an absolute or relative path, and the ~ character can be used to represent the user profile folder (which is platform-agnostic).

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: fetch
staticCommands.description: The fetch command clones or pulls all the repositories in the projects file in your local environment. If a repository does not exist locally, it will be cloned from the remote repository. If the repository already exists locally, a `git pull` will be performed to update it with the latest changes.

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: init
staticCommands.description: Initializes a new local git repository and pushes it to a remote repository.

values.name: repository
values.description: The URL of the empty remote repository.

values.name: default-branch-name
values.description: The name of the default branch for the repository.

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: install
staticCommands.description: Install a specific package, packages for the specified package manager, or all package managers if none is specified.

values.name: package-manager
values.description: The package manager to install packages for (e.g., pip, npm).

values.name: package
values.description: The package to install and add to the package manager repository.

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: link
staticCommands.description: Create symbolic links between files or folders

values.name: source
values.description: The source path of the file or directory

values.name: target
values.description: The target path of the new link

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: sync
staticCommands.description: Keep your files up to date and synchronized across multiple machines.

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning

staticCommands.name: update
staticCommands.description: Updates all packages for the specified package manager, or all package managers if none is specified.

values.name: package-manager
values.description: The package manager to update packages for (e.g., pip, npm).

options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are Trace, Debug, Information and Warning
